summary: Apple's lawyers informed Epic that Fortnite's developer account will not be reinstated until all court appeals are exhausted, potentially a five-year process. Gruber analyzes the exchange of letters between the two companies, noting that while Epic's Tim Sweeney publicly cried foul, his own communications to Apple contained conditions that effectively demanded Apple interpret the court's anti-steering injunction in Epic's favor — despite Epic having lost the lawsuit. Gruber points out that Sweeney deliberately muddled the situation by conflating Epic's multiple developer accounts, when in reality only the Fortnite-specific account was ever disabled. Epic's other accounts, including the crucial Unreal Engine account, remain operational by Apple's choice.
key_points: Apple refused to reinstate Epic's Fortnite developer account, citing Epic's past deception and Sweeney's public statements suggesting continued noncompliance
Sweeney's email to Phil Schiller promised compliance but attached conditions requiring Apple to interpret the court ruling favorably for Epic — the losing party
Only the Fortnite-specific developer account was ever disabled; Epic's other accounts including Unreal Engine remain active
Sweeney's public framing deliberately conflated the Fortnite account situation with Epic's broader developer relationship with Apple
The court ruling affirmed Apple would be within its rights to disable all of Epic's accounts, making Apple's restraint a deliberate choice
money_quotes: That's lawyer-speak, I believe, for 'go fuck yourselves'.
Sweeney is arguing that Apple, which won the lawsuit, should interpret the court's anti-steering injunction in a way that pleases Epic, which lost the lawsuit. That's not how things work.
Tim Sweeney is high as a kite.
Epic, of course, had previously promised the same thing, in a legally binding fashion, when they agreed to Apple's developer account terms and conditions.
Thank goodness lawyers seemingly always let you know in ALL CAPS when they send a letter via email, that they in fact SENT IT VIA ELECTRONIC MAIL.
That doesn't sound like the words of a man poised to comply with the App Store rules around in-app purchases.
key_insight: Epic's public outrage over Apple refusing to reinstate Fortnite obscures that Epic's own reinstatement request came loaded with conditions demanding the lawsuit's winner interpret the ruling to benefit the loser.
